title: Reader Response Calibration Samples
description: Reader-panel response samples used to calibrate automated engagement scores against actual reader reactions.

required_genres.description: Genres that must have reader-panel coverage before automated engagement thresholds can be retuned.

required_target_reader_segments.description: Named target-reader quota cells that must each have enough respondents before reader-response samples can retune hard gates.

minimum_respondents_per_required_target_.description: Minimum respondents required in each named target-reader quota cell.

require_target_reader_segment_quotas.description: Whether each sample must include target_reader_segment_counts for the required target-reader segments before gate retuning.

required_recruitment_channels.description: Named recruitment channels that must each contribute enough respondents before reader-response samples can retune hard gates.

minimum_respondents_per_required_recruit.description: Minimum respondents required in each named recruitment channel.

maximum_dominant_recruitment_channel_rat.description: Maximum allowed share of respondents from the single largest recruitment channel before panel protocol is considered source-skewed.

require_recruitment_channel_diversity.description: Whether samples must include recruitment_channel_counts for required recruitment channels before gate retuning.

maximum_samples_per_respondent.description: Maximum manuscript or variant samples a single respondent may evaluate before repeated-exposure fatigue or carryover risk blocks gate retuning.

minimum_order_balance_ratio.description: Minimum acceptable balance for manuscript/sample presentation order across repeated-exposure respondents before gate retuning.

minimum_samples_per_genre.description: Minimum total reader-panel samples required for each required genre.

minimum_usable_samples_per_genre.description: Minimum usable reader-panel samples required for each required genre.

minimum_human_respondent_ratio.description: Minimum share of respondents that must be verified human readers before samples can be used for hard gate tuning. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

require_human_reader_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need respondent_source and human respondent provenance before automated thresholds can be retuned.

require_response_data_quality.description: Whether reader-panel samples need response quality flags before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_median_read_time_seconds.description: Minimum median time respondents should spend reading/answering the manuscript sample before response data is considered credible.

maximum_median_reading_words_per_minute.description: Maximum plausible median reading speed after normalizing the manuscript sample's word count by median read time.

maximum_minimum_reading_words_per_minute.description: Maximum plausible fastest observed reading speed after normalizing the manuscript sample's word count by minimum read time.

maximum_median_reading_characters_per_mi.description: Maximum plausible median reading speed after normalizing the manuscript sample's character count by median read time.

maximum_minimum_reading_characters_per_m.description: Maximum plausible fastest observed reading speed after normalizing the manuscript sample's character count by minimum read time.

require_length_normalized_read_time.description: Whether response data-quality evidence must include manuscript_word_count or manuscript_character_count so reading time can be normalized by sample length.

maximum_speeding_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of respondents flagged as speeders.

maximum_straight_lining_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of respondents giving straight-lined or near-identical rating batteries.

maximum_duplicate_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of duplicate or near-duplicate responses.

maximum_bot_suspected_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of bot-suspected or automation-suspected responses.

maximum_low_quality_open_ended_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of low-quality open-ended responses.

maximum_inconsistent_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of inconsistent responses across repeated, validation, or cross-check items.

maximum_quality_flagged_response_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of unique respondents carrying any response-quality flag.

required_series_length.description: Minimum longest consecutive chapter run required for each required genre.

required_usable_series_length.description: Minimum longest consecutive usable chapter run required for each required genre.

minimum_holdout_samples.description: Minimum reserved holdout reader-panel samples required before automated engagement thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_usable_holdout_samples.description: Minimum usable reserved holdout reader-panel samples required before automated engagement thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_started_read_count.description: Minimum readers who started the sample before completed-reader averages can be trusted for gate tuning.

minimum_panel_completion_rate.description: Minimum share of started readers who completed the sample before rating. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

maximum_drop_off_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of started readers who dropped off before finishing. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

maximum_skimmed_read_ratio.description: Maximum allowed share of started readers who skimmed rather than fully read the sample. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

require_retention_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need started/completed/drop-off/skimming evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_drop_off_annotations.description: Minimum localized drop-off, skimming, slowdown, or mind-wandering annotations required when readers abandon or skim a sample.

minimum_actionable_drop_off_annotations.description: Minimum localized drop-off annotations that include a location, reason, and suggested revision.

require_drop_off_localization_evidence.description: Whether samples with observed drop-off or skimming must localize the exact scene, paragraph, sentence, or beat before threshold tuning.

minimum_annotation_coder_count.description: Minimum independent coders required before free-response friction annotations can be used for hard gate tuning.

minimum_annotation_double_coded_count.description: Minimum reader responses or annotation units that must be independently double-coded.

minimum_annotation_agreement_rate.description: Minimum inter-coder agreement or reliability rate required for annotation coding evidence. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

require_annotation_reliability_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need annotation coding reliability evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_unprompted_scene_recall_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must recall at least one scene moment without prompted choices before the sample can be used for gate tuning. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_distinctive_scene_recall_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share whose recall includes a concrete, chapter-specific image, choice, reversal, or consequence. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_scene_recall_annotations.description: Minimum structured scene_recall_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_scene_recall_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need unprompted scene recall evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_tension_trace_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must provide beat-level tension trace evidence before the sample can be used for gate tuning. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_tension_peak_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share whose trace marks a concrete suspense, curiosity, or surprise peak. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_tension_question_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share whose trace names the active narrative question, stake, or risk behind the tension. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_tension_trace_annotations.description: Minimum structured tension_trace_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_tension_trace_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need scene-level tension trace evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_forecast_prediction_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must provide narrative forecast predictions before the sample can be used for gate tuning. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_forecast_diversity_count.description: Minimum number of distinct reader predictions required to show that the story is not mechanically obvious.

minimum_forecast_revision_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share whose predictions changed while reading because the chapter shifted available evidence or stakes. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_forecast_mismatch_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share whose forecast diverged from the actual outcome in a meaningful, fair-play way. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_narrative_forecast_annotations.description: Minimum structured narrative_forecast_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_narrative_forecast_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need narrative forecast evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_quote_recall_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must remember or quote a specific line before the sample can be used for gate tuning. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_favorite_line_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must mark a line as a favorite, striking, or especially valuable sentence. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_shareable_line_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must identify a line they would quote, screenshot, share, or discuss with another reader. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_line_quote_annotations.description: Minimum structured line_quote_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_line_quote_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need memorable or quotable line evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_payoff_setup_recall_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must remember the setup, plant, or earlier clue that made a payoff feel prepared. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_payoff_trigger_recognition_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must identify the trigger, reveal, or connective beat that activated the payoff. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_payoff_earned_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must say the payoff felt earned rather than arbitrary or like a cheat. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_payoff_recontextualization_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that must report a changed interpretation of an earlier scene, clue, relationship, rule, or choice after the payoff.

minimum_payoff_emotional_satisfaction_ra.description: Minimum respondent share that must report emotional satisfaction, catharsis, dread, delight, or other payoff-specific feeling. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_payoff_fairness_annotations.description: Minimum structured payoff_fairness_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_payoff_fairness_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need setup/payoff fairness reconstruction evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_organic_recommendation_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that would organically recommend or mention the chapter to another target reader. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_discussion_prompt_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that identified a concrete question, dilemma, scene, or character choice they wanted to discuss.

minimum_advocacy_annotations.description: Minimum structured advocacy_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_advocacy_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need recommendation, sharing, or discussion evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_bookmark_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that would bookmark, save, follow, or add the work to a library. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_return_intent_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that would return after a delay or keep reading more chapters in the same session. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_paid_continuation_intent_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that would spend paid currency, subscription attention, or premium unlocks to continue. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_durable_engagement_annotations.description: Minimum structured durable_engagement_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_durable_engagement_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need bookmark, follow, return, binge, or paid-continuation evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_continuation_behavior_impression.description: Minimum next-chapter CTA impressions required before actual continuation behavior can be used for hard gate tuning.

minimum_next_chapter_click_through_ratio.description: Minimum share of next-chapter CTA impressions that should produce a next-chapter click. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_next_chapter_open_ratio.description: Minimum share of next-chapter CTA impressions that should produce a next-chapter open. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_next_chapter_read_start_ratio.description: Minimum share of next-chapter CTA impressions that should produce a next-chapter read start. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

require_continuation_behavior_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need actual next-chapter click/open/read-start behavior before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_lingering_emotion_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that reports a lingering emotion, mood shift, or emotional aftertaste after reading. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_reflective_meaning_ratio.description: Minimum respondent share that reports a reflective question, personal memory, changed interpretation, or meaning carried after reading. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_resonance_annotations.description: Minimum structured resonance_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_resonance_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need post-reading emotional resonance evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_delayed_follow_up_respondent_rat.description: Minimum share of original respondents who must complete a delayed follow-up before the sample can tune hard gates. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_delayed_follow_up_hours.description: Minimum delay in hours between the initial reading and follow-up memory check.

minimum_delayed_scene_recall_ratio.description: Minimum original-respondent share that must recall a concrete scene, image, line, or beat in the delayed follow-up. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_delayed_character_recall_ratio.description: Minimum original-respondent share that must recall a character, relationship, decision, or character-specific pressure in the delayed follow-up. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_delayed_continuation_intent_rati.description: Minimum original-respondent share that still reports next-click, return, or paid continuation intent in the delayed follow-up. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_delayed_memory_annotations.description: Minimum structured delayed_memory_annotations required before the sample can be used for hard gate tuning.

require_delayed_memory_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need delayed follow-up memory and continuation evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

require_comparative_preference.description: Whether every reader-panel sample must include strong blind pairwise preference evidence against a target-market reference before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_comparative_preference_win_rate.description: Minimum share of blind pairwise comparisons in which readers prefer the current manuscript over a target-market reference. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_comparative_preference_responden.description: Minimum respondents required for comparative preference evidence.

require_revision_outcome_evidence.description: Whether reader-panel samples need before/after revision outcome evidence before automated thresholds can be retuned.

minimum_revision_lift.description: Minimum reader-score improvement required between the baseline draft and revised draft.

minimum_revision_preference_win_rate.description: Minimum share of blind before/after pairwise comparisons in which readers prefer the revised draft. Accepts 0-1 ratios.

minimum_revision_preference_respondents.description: Minimum respondents required for revision preference evidence.

required_chapter_ranges.description: Chapter ranges that must have reader-panel coverage before automated engagement thresholds can be retuned.

calibration_split.description: Whether the sample is used for calibration/development, intermediate validation, or reserved holdout confirmation. Threshold retuning should be confirmed on holdout samples.

automated.description: Optional automated scores. If omitted, calibrate-reader-response reads meta/quality-trend.json by chapter/version.

reader.description: Reader-panel response dimensions. Scores can use 0-100 or the provided rating_scale, such as 1-7 Likert.

next_click.description: How strongly readers want to continue to the next chapter.

character_attachment.description: How much readers care about the protagonist or central character's outcome.

relationship_investment.description: How much readers care about the relationship turn, trust shift, or interpersonal stake.

novelty.description: How fresh, non-generic, or meaningfully different the premise, scene route, or payoff feels to readers.

surprise.description: How much readers feel an earned reversal, discovery, or non-obvious turn.

resonance.description: How much aftertaste, memory, emotional echo, or reflective meaning remains after reading.

scene_recall.description: How strongly readers can recall a concrete, distinctive scene moment without prompting.

recommendation_intent.description: How strongly readers would recommend, share, or discuss the chapter with another target reader.

bookmark_intent.description: How strongly readers would bookmark, save, follow, or add the work to their library.

return_intent.description: How strongly readers expect to return to the story after a delay.

purchase_intent.description: How strongly readers would spend paid currency, subscription attention, or a premium unlock to continue.

binge_intent.description: How strongly readers want to keep reading multiple chapters in the same sitting.

evidence.description: Evidence that the reader response sample is actionable enough for threshold tuning.

respondent_source.description: Provenance of the respondent data. Only verified human reader panels are usable for hard gate tuning by default.

human_respondent_count.description: Respondents verified as real human readers, excluding synthetic AI responses and author/system estimates.

synthetic_respondent_count.description: Responses generated or simulated by AI/synthetic respondents.

author_estimate_count.description: Responses that are author, editor, or system estimates rather than independent reader answers.

manuscript_word_count.description: Word count of the exact manuscript excerpt shown to the reader panel, used to normalize read-time plausibility.

manuscript_character_count.description: Character count of the exact manuscript excerpt shown to the reader panel, used to normalize Korean or whitespace-sensitive read-time plausibility.

median_read_time_seconds.description: Median time respondents spent reading/answering this manuscript sample.

minimum_read_time_seconds.description: Shortest observed completion time for this manuscript sample.

speeding_response_count.description: Respondents flagged for completing too quickly to have plausibly read the sample.

straight_lining_response_count.description: Respondents flagged for straight-lining or near-identical response batteries.

bot_suspected_response_count.description: Responses suspected to come from bots, automation, or non-human survey agents.

low_quality_open_ended_response_count.description: Open-ended answers that are nonsensical, template-like, copied, irrelevant, or too thin to support the rating.

inconsistent_response_count.description: Respondents flagged for inconsistent validation, repeated-item, or cross-check answers.

quality_flagged_response_count.description: Unique respondents carrying any data-quality flag after de-duplicating overlapping flags.

target_reader_count.description: Respondents who match the intended genre/platform reader segment.

target_reader_segment_count.description: Distinct intended-reader cohorts represented in the panel, such as genre fans, platform-native readers, expert readers, or style-preference groups.

target_reader_segment_counts.description: Respondent counts per named target-reader quota cell. Keys should match required_target_reader_segments when quota coverage is required.

dominant_reader_segment_ratio.description: Share of respondents from the largest reader cohort. High values indicate a narrow panel that may overfit one reader profile.

started_read_count.description: Readers who opened or began the sampled chapter, including readers who dropped off before rating.

completed_read_count.description: Respondents who finished the sampled chapter before rating.

drop_off_count.description: Readers who started the sample but stopped before finishing or before giving complete ratings.

skimmed_read_count.description: Readers who self-reported skimming or were flagged as skimming the sample.

qualitative_comment_count.description: Respondents who left usable free-response feedback.

friction_point_count.description: Distinct scene, paragraph, or sentence-level drop-off/friction annotations.

actionable_friction_point_count.description: Friction annotations tied to a specific location and reason strongly enough to drive revision.

rewrite_suggestion_count.description: Concrete rewrite directives or revision suggestions derived from reader feedback.

friction_annotations.description: Structured scene, paragraph, or sentence-level reader friction notes. These turn reader-panel scores into revision directives.

location.description: Chapter-local location such as scene id, paragraph number, sentence number, or beat label.

quote.description: Short reader quote or short manuscript excerpt identifying the friction point.

reason.description: Why readers stopped, drifted, felt unconvinced, or lost desire to continue.

rewrite_suggestion.description: Concrete revision action derived from the reader feedback.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who marked this or equivalent friction.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this annotation, such as genre-core, platform-native, style-sensitive, or expert-reader.

drop_off_annotations.description: Localized reader drop-off, skimming, slowdown, or mind-wandering events. Required when retention counts show readers left or skimmed.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, sentence, or beat where the reader stopped, skimmed, slowed down, or lost focus.

last_completed_location.description: Last scene, paragraph, sentence, or beat the reader reported finishing before disengaging.

trigger_quote.description: Short reader quote or manuscript excerpt that identifies the disengagement trigger.

reason.description: Why the reader stopped, skimmed, slowed down, or reported mind-wandering at this location.

reader_count.description: Number of readers who produced this or an equivalent disengagement event.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this event.

suggested_revision.description: Concrete revision action that addresses the localized disengagement trigger.

annotation_coder_count.description: Independent human coders who assigned issue labels, dimensions, or rewrite directives to reader free-response annotations.

annotation_double_coded_count.description: Reader responses or annotation units independently coded by at least two coders.

annotation_agreement_rate.description: Agreement or reliability value for the annotation coding metric, normalized to a 0-1 ratio.

annotation_reliability_metric.description: Inter-coder agreement or reliability metric used for reader annotation coding.

annotation_codebook_version.description: Version or identifier of the annotation codebook used by coders.

annotation_adjudicated.description: Whether coder disagreements were reviewed and resolved before turning annotations into revision directives.

annotation_coder_blinded.description: Whether coders were blinded to automated scores, author/model identity, or expected verdict while coding responses.

unprompted_scene_recall_count.description: Respondents who could name at least one remembered scene moment without being shown answer choices.

distinctive_scene_recall_count.description: Respondents whose unprompted recall included a concrete image, choice, reversal, consequence, or other distinctive chapter-specific detail.

scene_recall_annotations.description: Structured positive reader-memory notes that identify what scene moments readers could recall after reading.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or closing image that readers remembered.

remembered_moment.description: What readers recalled in their own words or as a normalized recall label.

distinctive_detail.description: Concrete detail that makes the recalled scene chapter-specific rather than a generic plot summary.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who recalled this or equivalent moment.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this recall annotation.

tension_trace_point_count.description: Respondents who marked at least one beat-level tension, curiosity, or suspense point while reading.

tension_peak_count.description: Respondents whose trace identified at least one concrete high-tension peak.

tension_question_count.description: Respondents whose trace named the active narrative question, stake, risk, or uncertainty behind the tension.

tension_trace_annotations.description: Structured beat-level reader tension traces that identify where suspense, curiosity, surprise, or risk rose while reading.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or sentence where readers felt tension shift.

experienced_tension.description: What readers felt becoming suspenseful, uncertain, risky, or surprising at this point.

narrative_question.description: Question readers wanted answered because of this beat.

stake_or_risk.description: Concrete danger, cost, relationship risk, time pressure, or uncertainty readers perceived.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who marked this or equivalent tension point.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this tension trace annotation.

reason.description: Why this beat raised, released, or failed to raise tension for readers.

forecast_prediction_count.description: Respondents who made at least one concrete prediction about the chapter or near-future story outcome while reading.

forecast_diversity_count.description: Distinct prediction clusters observed in reader forecasts, used to detect whether tension is too mechanically obvious.

forecast_revision_count.description: Respondents whose forecast changed after a clue, reversal, choice, or escalation shifted the story model.

forecast_mismatch_count.description: Respondents whose forecast differed from the actual outcome in a meaningful, fair-play way.

forecast_inflection_count.description: Respondents whose forecast curve or certainty changed at a concrete narrative beat.

narrative_forecast_annotations.description: Structured reader forecast traces that show initial predictions, revised predictions, actual outcomes, and why forecast mismatch or change created tension.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or sentence where the forecast was made or changed.

initial_prediction.description: What readers initially expected would happen.

revised_prediction.description: How readers updated their prediction after new evidence, a reversal, or a pressure beat.

actual_outcome.description: What actually happened in the chapter or validated follow-up beat.

prediction_mismatch.description: Whether the prediction meaningfully diverged from the actual outcome without becoming arbitrary.

prediction_shift.description: The clue, choice, reversal, or escalation that changed the reader forecast.

surprise_or_tension_reason.description: Why the prediction update or mismatch created suspense, curiosity, surprise, or reader investment.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who made this or equivalent forecast.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this forecast annotation.

quote_recall_count.description: Respondents who could recall, quote, or identify at least one specific line from the chapter.

favorite_line_count.description: Respondents who marked a line as a favorite, striking, beautiful, funny, emotionally sharp, or otherwise especially valuable.

shareable_line_count.description: Respondents who said they would quote, screenshot, share, or discuss a specific line.

line_quote_annotations.description: Structured reader notes for memorable, favorite, or shareable lines that preserve the exact line and why it worked.

location.description: Chapter-local sentence, paragraph, beat, or scene containing the quoted line.

quoted_line.description: The exact or near-exact line readers remembered, selected, or wanted to quote.

appeal_reason.description: Why the line worked for readers: voice, image, emotion, theme, wit, character pressure, or payoff.

share_reason.description: Why readers would quote, screenshot, share, or discuss the line.

line_function.description: Primary craft function readers attributed to the line.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who selected this or an equivalent line.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this line quote annotation.

payoff_setup_recall_count.description: Respondents who could name the setup, plant, clue, earlier scene, relationship beat, or rule that prepared the payoff.

payoff_trigger_recognition_count.description: Respondents who identified the trigger, reveal, or connective beat that activated the payoff.

payoff_earned_count.description: Respondents who said the payoff felt earned, fair, or properly prepared rather than arbitrary.

payoff_recontextualization_count.description: Respondents who reported that the payoff changed how they understood an earlier clue, scene, relationship, rule, or choice.

payoff_emotional_satisfaction_count.description: Respondents who reported payoff-specific emotional satisfaction, catharsis, dread, delight, or release.

payoff_fairness_annotations.description: Structured reader notes that reconstruct whether a payoff was prepared, triggered, earned, emotionally satisfying, and able to recontextualize earlier story evidence.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or sentence where the payoff occurred.

payoff_moment.description: The reveal, reversal, reward, emotional turn, clue resolution, or consequence readers treated as the payoff.

remembered_setup.description: The earlier setup, plant, image, clue, relationship beat, rule, or choice readers remembered after the payoff.

trigger_or_reveal.description: The trigger, reveal, or connective beat that made readers connect setup to payoff.

changed_interpretation.description: How the payoff changed readers' interpretation of earlier evidence.

earned_reason.description: Why readers felt the payoff was earned, fair, or emotionally prepared.

arbitrary_or_cheat_reason.description: Why readers felt the payoff was arbitrary, unearned, too hidden, contradicted prior evidence, or cheated.

emotional_payoff_reason.description: The satisfaction, catharsis, dread, delight, relief, or emotional release generated by the payoff.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who made this or equivalent payoff fairness observation.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this payoff fairness annotation.

organic_recommendation_count.description: Respondents who said they would organically recommend, mention, share, or pitch the chapter to another target reader.

discussion_prompt_count.description: Respondents who identified a concrete scene, question, dilemma, or character choice they wanted to discuss.

advocacy_annotations.description: Structured positive reader-advocacy notes that identify why readers would recommend, share, or discuss the chapter.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or closing image that generated recommendation or discussion intent.

share_trigger.description: What readers would tell another target reader about this chapter.

recommended_audience.description: Specific friend, reader cohort, trope fan, or comparable audience the respondent would recommend it to.

discussion_prompt.description: Question, dilemma, reversal, or character choice readers wanted to talk about after reading.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who gave this or equivalent advocacy reason.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this advocacy annotation.

bookmark_count.description: Respondents who would bookmark, save, or mark the work for later continuation.

follow_or_library_add_count.description: Respondents who would follow the work, subscribe, or add it to a platform library.

return_next_day_count.description: Respondents who said they would return after a delay, such as the next day or next update.

binge_read_intent_count.description: Respondents who said they wanted to keep reading multiple chapters in the same sitting.

paid_continuation_intent_count.description: Respondents who would spend paid currency, subscription attention, or a premium unlock to continue.

durable_engagement_annotations.description: Structured positive commitment notes that identify why readers would bookmark, follow, return, binge, or pay to continue.

location.description: Chapter-local scene, paragraph, beat, or closing image that generated durable engagement.

commitment_trigger.description: What made readers want to save, follow, return, binge, or pay to continue.

intended_action.description: Reader action implied by this commitment trigger.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who gave this or equivalent durable engagement reason.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this durable engagement annotation.

reason.description: Why this moment creates a stronger continuation commitment than ordinary liking.

next_chapter_cta_impression_count.description: Readers who were shown a next-chapter continuation CTA after finishing the sampled chapter.

next_chapter_click_count.description: Readers who clicked the next-chapter CTA after finishing the sampled chapter.

next_chapter_open_count.description: Readers who opened the next chapter after the sampled chapter.

next_chapter_read_start_count.description: Readers who started reading the next chapter after opening or clicking through from the sampled chapter.

lingering_emotion_count.description: Respondents who reported a lingering emotion, mood shift, or emotional aftertaste after reading.

reflective_comment_count.description: Respondents who reported a reflective question, theme, moral tension, or changed interpretation after reading.

personal_memory_or_meaning_count.description: Respondents who connected the chapter to a personal memory, relationship, fear, desire, or self-understanding.

resonance_annotations.description: Structured post-reading emotional resonance notes that explain what stayed with readers after the chapter ended.

location.description: Scene, paragraph, image, line, or beat that produced the aftertaste.

lingering_emotion.description: Emotion or mood readers carried after closing the chapter.

reflective_question.description: Question, dilemma, theme, or interpretation readers kept thinking about.

remembered_image.description: Concrete image, line, object, gesture, or sensory detail readers remembered as the aftertaste anchor.

personal_meaning.description: Personal memory, life comparison, self-understanding, or relationship meaning readers connected to the chapter.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who gave this or equivalent resonance note.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this resonance annotation.

delayed_follow_up_respondent_count.description: Original respondents who completed a delayed memory follow-up after the initial reading.

delayed_follow_up_hours.description: Hours between the initial reading and delayed memory follow-up.

delayed_scene_recall_count.description: Delayed follow-up respondents who still recalled a concrete scene, image, line, object, gesture, or beat.

delayed_character_recall_count.description: Delayed follow-up respondents who still recalled a character, relationship, decision, wound, cost, or character-specific pressure.

delayed_next_click_intent_count.description: Delayed follow-up respondents who still wanted to click the next chapter.

delayed_return_intent_count.description: Delayed follow-up respondents who still intended to return later for the story.

delayed_paid_continuation_intent_count.description: Delayed follow-up respondents who still reported paid unlock, subscription, or premium continuation intent.

delayed_memory_annotations.description: Structured delayed follow-up notes that preserve what readers remembered and why they still wanted to continue.

location.description: Scene, paragraph, image, line, or beat remembered in delayed follow-up.

delayed_remembered_moment.description: What the reader still remembered after the delay.

delayed_character_or_relationship.description: Character, relationship, decision, wound, or cost that stayed memorable after the delay.

delayed_next_question.description: Question or unresolved pressure that still made the reader want the next chapter.

return_or_purchase_reason.description: Reason the reader still intended to return, bookmark, subscribe, or pay for continuation after the delay.

reader_count.description: Number of respondents who gave this or equivalent delayed memory note.

reader_segment.description: Target-reader cohort that produced this delayed memory annotation.

reader_score_standard_deviation.description: Standard deviation of reader composite or overall scores. Uses rating_scale units when rating_scale is present; CLI normalizes it to 0-100. Also used with respondent_count to calculate the 95% reader score margin of error.

high_response_count.description: Respondents whose overall response is high enough to count as positive.

neutral_response_count.description: Respondents whose overall response is neither clearly positive nor clearly negative.

low_response_count.description: Respondents whose overall response is low enough to count as negative.

blind_reading.description: Whether readers saw the manuscript without author/model/source labels that could bias response.

author_identity_masked.description: Whether author, title, platform, model, or source identity cues were masked before readers evaluated the manuscript.

prior_exposure_screened.description: Whether respondents were screened for prior exposure to the manuscript, earlier versions, or public excerpts.

unexcluded_prior_exposure_count.description: Respondents with prior manuscript/version exposure who remained in the analyzed sample after exclusions.

spoiler_exposure_screened.description: Whether respondents were screened for spoiler, synopsis, outline, or outside plot-summary exposure before rating the manuscript.

unexcluded_spoiler_exposure_count.description: Respondents with spoiler, synopsis, or outside plot-summary exposure who remained in the analyzed sample after exclusions.

neutral_question_wording.description: Whether survey questions used neutral wording and avoided leading readers toward positive or negative reactions.

response_option_order_randomized.description: Whether response options or item order were randomized/counterbalanced to reduce order effects.

sample_order_randomized.description: Whether manuscript/sample presentation order was randomized for respondents who evaluated more than one sample.

manuscript_order_counterbalanced.description: Whether manuscript, chapter, or variant order was counterbalanced across respondents to reduce carryover and fatigue effects.

max_samples_per_respondent.description: Maximum manuscript or variant samples assigned to any single respondent in this reader-panel sample.

order_balance_ratio.description: Observed manuscript/sample order balance ratio across repeated-exposure respondents.

question_wording_disclosed.description: Whether the exact survey question wording, response options, and reader instructions are preserved with the sample.

recruitment_method_disclosed.description: Whether recruitment source, sampling method, and target-reader screening rules are documented.

recruitment_channel_counts.description: Respondent counts per named recruitment channel, such as opt-in panel, newsletter, platform community, or external beta-reader pool. Totals should match respondent_count.

population_definition_disclosed.description: Whether the target reader population and eligibility criteria are defined for this reader-panel sample.

sampling_frame_disclosed.description: Whether the sampling frame, panel source, or source coverage is documented.

fieldwork_dates_disclosed.description: Whether the dates of reader response data collection are preserved with the sample.

survey_mode_disclosed.description: Whether survey mode, reading environment, or administration channel is documented.

incentive_disclosed.description: Whether respondent incentives, compensation, or no-incentive conditions are documented.

attention_check_pass_count.description: Respondents who passed attention or quality checks before inclusion.

excluded_response_count.description: Responses excluded for duplicate, incomplete, inattentive, or off-target participation.

comparative_reference_label.description: Reference chapter, market comp, or alternate draft used in blind pairwise comparison.

comparative_preference_win_rate.description: Share of pairwise comparisons in which readers preferred this manuscript over the reference, with ties optionally counted as half-wins before entry.

comparative_preference_current_count.description: Respondents who preferred this manuscript in the pairwise comparison.

comparative_preference_reference_count.description: Respondents who preferred the reference manuscript in the pairwise comparison.

comparative_preference_tie_count.description: Respondents who marked the pairwise comparison as tied or no clear preference.

comparative_preference_respondent_count.description: Respondents included in the comparative preference test.

comparative_blind_pairwise.description: Whether the comparison hid source, author/model, and reference labels from readers.

comparative_same_reader_cohort.description: Whether the same target-reader cohort rated both the manuscript and reference.

comparative_question_wording_disclosed.description: Whether the exact pairwise preference question wording is preserved with this sample.

revision_pair_id.description: Identifier linking the baseline draft and revised draft being compared.

revision_baseline_reader_score.description: Reader composite score for the baseline draft before applying revision directives.

revision_current_reader_score.description: Reader composite score for the revised draft. If omitted, the current sample composite is used.

revision_preference_revised_count.description: Respondents who preferred the revised draft in a before/after pairwise comparison.

revision_preference_baseline_count.description: Respondents who preferred the baseline draft in a before/after pairwise comparison.

revision_preference_tie_count.description: Respondents who marked the baseline/revised comparison as tied or no clear preference.

revision_preference_respondent_count.description: Respondents included in the baseline/revised revision outcome comparison.

revision_blind_comparison.description: Whether readers compared baseline and revised drafts without knowing which was revised.

revision_same_reader_cohort.description: Whether the same or matched target-reader cohort rated both baseline and revised drafts.

revision_question_wording_disclosed.description: Whether the exact before/after revision comparison question wording is preserved with this sample.

revision_guardrail_regression_count.description: Respondents or tracked dimensions showing the revision harmed a guardrail dimension such as clarity, character attachment, payoff fairness, or style comfort.
